spans.text: round the foot of every steep Whose cliffs above the ocean grow
the flowery banks , And sing my name , and garlands
to prove my charmful power While floating on the moonlight wave
my charmful power While floating on the moonlight wave
wake the magic of my shell , And choral voices round me rise
The trembling youth , charmed by my strain
our ditties sweet To some soft shell that warbles near
My way by quivering lightnings shown
My way by quivering lightnings shown
lofty groves , That wild upon the sea-bank wave ; Where sweet fruits bloom , and fresh spring roves , And closing boughs the tempest brave
the sea-bank wave ; Where sweet fruits bloom , and fresh spring
My potent voice they love so well
Whoever you are that love my lay
the wave , To the still sands , where fairies
